EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Slick roads were blamed for a multi-vehicle collision on State Highway 31 near Victor.  Six individual automobiles were involved with no major injuries reported.  The highway was blocked in both directions for around 2 and a half hours.  The accident occurred shortly after 11 AM on January 3rd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Slick roads were blamed for a multi-vehicle collision on State Highway 31 near Victor.  Six individual automobiles were involved with no major injuries reported.  The highway was blocked in both directions for around 2 and a half hours.  The accident occurred shortly after 11 AM on January 3rd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Some SNOTEL sites estimated over 10 inches of snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Estimated snowfall amounts were 8 inches at Emigrant Summit and 15 inches at Franklin Basin.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Some SNOTEL sites estimated over 10 inches of snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An estimated 13 inches of snow was estimated at Vienna Mine.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: An estimated 13 inches of snow fell at Lost Wood Divide.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Some SNOTEL sites estimated over 10 inches of snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An estimated 10 inches of snow fell at Bear Canyon and 13 at Smiley Mountain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts of 50 to 65 mph widespread on the 8th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread 50 to 65 mph wind gusts measured on the 8th with a peak of 62 mph recorded at the Yale interstate 84 site 7 miles north northwest of Idahome.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An atmospheric river brought heavy snow to pass level locations and moderate snow to higher valleys of north-central Idaho.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mountain SNOTEL gauges reported 1.8 to 2.1 inches of precipitation, which fell as 12 to 14 inches of snow. Below approximately 4000 feet, a wintry mix occurred, resulting in slick roadways.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An atmospheric river brought heavy snow to pass level locations and moderate snow to higher valleys of north-central Idaho.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wet snow caused power outages for approximately 900 customers near Fenn and southwest of Elk City. SNOTEL measurements at Lolo Pass recorded 1.3 inches of moisture, resulting in 13 inches of new snow. These conditions led to chain requirements at Lolo Pass for a period during the evening of the 6th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cold temperatures resulted in a gradually build up of ice and water backing up on the Lemhi River between Tendoy and Lemhi.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Lemhi River exceeded action stage on the 27th and reached minor flood stage on the 28th. This caused yard flooding south of Tendoy, between Mabee Lane and McDevitt Creek Road, though no homes were impacted. The river crested at 7.07 feet at 2:00 PM MST on the 29th at the Lemhi River near Lemhi gauge, then receded that evening as ice broke up.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Relatively warm and moist air riding over cold valleys brought rain, freezing rain, and snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A light wintry mix resulted in road conditions described as slick as can be on Highway 14 in Elk City. Light rain fell while temperatures remained near freezing for several locations, including Elk City and near Syringa.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front pushed a band of heavy snow through the North Idaho Panhandle on late January 7. It brought widespread snowfall amounts ranging from 4 to 13 inches. Lookout Pass received 21 inches of new snow for the period January 6 through 8.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Observer reported 4 inches of snow 5 miles South of Moscow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front pushed a band of heavy snow through the North Idaho Panhandle on late January 7. It brought widespread snowfall amounts ranging from 4 to 13 inches. Lookout Pass received 21 inches of new snow for the period January 6 through 8.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Observer reported 8 inches of snow 2 miles Northeast of Hayden Lake. Other reports from the area ranged 4 to 9 inches for Rathdrum, Post Falls, and Hayden.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front pushed a band of heavy snow through the North Idaho Panhandle on late January 7. It brought widespread snowfall amounts ranging from 4 to 13 inches. Lookout Pass received 21 inches of new snow for the period January 6 through 8.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Observer reported an estimated 13 inches of snow in Hauser. Time is based off of Sandpoint Airport weather station. Athol had a range of 4 to 5 inches of snow. Spirit Lake received 5 to 6 inches of snow.
